title: Post-stop recovery context
summary: Use current and past `PitStop`/fresh-tyre indicators to identify rows immediately after a stop and the first few laps of a new stint.
rationale: The target is next-lap stopping, so rows just after a stop should usually carry a different risk profile from old-stint rows. The current-lap pit flag is an available feature, not the next-lap target.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, derive current stop observed, previous lap stop observed, laps since last observed stop, fresh-tyre-after-stop flags, and interactions between post-stop state and `TyreLife`, `Compound`, and `RaceProgress`.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Improper shifting could leak future stops; only use current and earlier rows.
